title: Test ORM Database Composition on PostgreSQL
description:
Practical testing phase for database composability. Apply existing ORM code to PostgreSQL and attempt to build the database from ORM definitions without migrations. Compare the composed database with the migration-built version for consistency.

### Outcome
Based on findings from MBT-1179, the composability approach was determined to not be beneficial for this project. Migrations remain the recommended approach.
acceptance_criteria: ORM code applied to PostgreSQL database
data_model_changes: Testing ORM-based database composition vs migration-based approach.
implementation_notes: As concluded in MBT-1179, the compose option is not better than the existing migration approach. Migrations are retained.
comment_summary: Developer confirmed that as reviewed in MBT-1179, the composability option is not suitable. Sticking with migrations.
